claim: The local Krea2 snapshot is already Krea-2-Turbo, so the first speed lever is runtime placement rather than switching to another Turbo checkpoint.
weight_reason: The exact local folder is Krea-2-Turbo and official Krea/Diffusers docs identify the Turbo pipeline and intended fast inference path.

claim: The local Krea2 component footprint is dominated by the transformer at about 24.48 GiB and text encoder at about 8.27 GiB, while the VAE is only about 0.47 GiB, so replacing the VAE is not the primary speed or VRAM lever.
weight_reason: Direct local component-size scan of the model snapshot.

claim: Official Diffusers memory guidance describes sequential CPU offload as the lowest-memory option but slower, while group offload with streaming is a middle option between sequential offload and fully resident GPU placement.
weight_reason: Official Diffusers memory optimization docs describe these tradeoffs and APIs.

claim: On this workstation, the old lowest-memory sequential path produced a 512x512 warm run of 34.05 seconds after the first 102.79 second run, with about 1.29 GiB peak PyTorch allocation.
weight_reason: Local repeat smoke receipt from the actual AIWF Krea2 backend.

claim: On this workstation, the new mid/normal streamed group-offload policy produced a 512x512 warm run of 29.37 seconds after the first 71.04 second run, with about 4.91 GiB PyTorch reserved and about 5.68 GiB observed by nvidia-smi.
weight_reason: Local repeat smoke receipt from the tracked mid-profile policy path.

claim: On this workstation, the high profile with FP8 layerwise resident transformer and CPU text encoding produced a 512x512 warm run of 8.11 seconds after the first 59.42 second run, with about 14.69 GiB observed by nvidia-smi.
weight_reason: Local repeat smoke receipt from the tracked high-profile policy path.

claim: Krea's official repository says LoRAs trained on the Raw model can be applied to Turbo, which makes adapters the practical customization path before trying incompatible text encoder swaps.
weight_reason: Official model-author repo guidance, with the runtime exposing LoRA adapter APIs locally.

claim: Replacing the Krea2 text encoder is compatibility-sensitive because Krea2 uses the Qwen3-VL text encoder contract; the safer near-term approach is prompt-embedding cache plus CPU/GPU placement control rather than arbitrary text-encoder substitution.
weight_reason: Official Krea2 docs describe the Qwen3-VL encoder and local runtime inspection confirms the pipeline component contract.

claim: The high FP8-resident profile is not yet safe as a broad larger-image default on this 16 GB card: a 768x768 repeat smoke exceeded the 5-minute command timeout, sat near 16.0 GiB used, and was stopped before completing run 1.
weight_reason: Direct local negative smoke receipt after the high-profile policy change.

claim: The mid streamed group-offload profile is the reliable 768x768 Krea2 path on this card in this pass: it completed two 8-step runs, with a 34.20 second warm run and about 8.02 GiB observed by nvidia-smi.
weight_reason: Direct local repeat smoke receipt from the tracked mid-profile policy path.
